connections.name: Virtual Desktop
requirements: Virtual Desktop purchased on Pico Store

knownIssues.title: Pico Connect has higher overhead than Virtual Desktop
knownIssues.description: Pico's official streaming software tends to use more CPU and has higher latency than third-party alternatives like Virtual Desktop.
knownIssues.workaround: Try Virtual Desktop or ALVR for lower latency. Pico Connect works fine but VD is generally smoother.

knownIssues.title: Controller tracking loses accuracy at extreme angles
knownIssues.description: Inside-out tracking on the Pico 4 can lose controllers when they're behind you or at extreme angles from the headset cameras.
knownIssues.workaround: Keep controllers within the headset's camera FOV. For full-body tracking games, consider adding external trackers.

optimizationTips.tip: Use Virtual Desktop for the best wireless experience
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: Virtual Desktop tends to give the smoothest and most responsive wireless VR on the Pico 4.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: Virtual Desktop's SSW (Synchronous Spacewarp) provides better reprojection than Pico Connect's native ASW. Combined with lower encode latency, VD typically achieves 5-10ms lower motion-to-photon than Pico Connect on the same network.

optimizationTips.tip: Use SteamVR at 90Hz for best stability
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: Running at 90Hz gives you smooth VR that works well with most PCs.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: The Pico 4's 90Hz mode provides 11.1ms frame budget — achievable on mid-range GPUs (RTX 2070+) with SteamVR SS at 100%. The Pico 4 does not support 120Hz in PCVR streaming mode.
